Meta.Purpose: POTOP is a 3D turret defense and survival game featuring two modes: defensive turret combat and obstacle-based survival.
Meta.Architecture: Singleton-based Global State Management with Event-Driven UI (MVC/MVP influence).

Dependencies.Package: New Input System for player controls and interactions.
Legacy UI system components (TextMeshPro, Sliders) used in HUD.
Integration for Model Context Protocol in Unity.

CoreScenes.Scene: Main menu for game mode selection and application exit logic.
Turret defense gameplay featuring enemy spawning and combat.
Survival mode where players dodge/destroy obstacles while accumulating survival time.

Module.name: GameManager
Module.Responsibility: Singleton managing HP, score, game state transitions, and global gameplay events.

Module.name: GameHUD
Module.Responsibility: UI controller subscribing to GameManager events to update HUD elements and Game Over screen.

Module.name: StartMain
Module.Responsibility: Menu entry point managing scene transitions between Turret and Stage modes.

Module.name: StageMain
Module.Responsibility: Survival mode logic managing survival time scores and obstacle spawning frequency.

Module.name: TurretShooter
Module.Responsibility: Handles player input for turret rotation and projectile instantiation during combat.

Module.name: EnemySpawner
Module.Responsibility: Automates enemy generation and positioning in turret defense mode.

DataFlowAndState.Description: Global state (HP, Score, GameOver) is centralized in the GameManager singleton. Gameplay entities (Enemies, Players) call TakeDamage/AddScore on the singleton, which updates state and triggers static C# Actions. UI components (GameHUD) subscribe to these events on Enable/Disable for decoupled visual updates.

StrictConventions.Rule: Rely on GameManager.Instance for global state; avoid direct cross-script coupling.
Use static events (Actions) in Managers to notify UI and other systems of state changes.
Strictly avoid GameObject.Find(); use Inspector references, Singletons, or Dependency Injection.
Declare all access modifiers explicitly; use [SerializeField] for private fields exposed to Inspector.
Prefer Unity UI Toolkit for new UI development (as per AGENTS.md), though legacy UGUI exists.
